title: eryngium × intermedium
intro: Eryngium × intermedium, also known as the Intermediate Sea Holly, is a hybrid plant species within the Eryngium genus, characterized by its striking, silvery-gray leaves and vibrant blue or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Eryngium × intermedium include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and other infections. Eryngium × intermedium is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusions, and can be used externally as a poultice or compress to aid in the healing of wounds and skin irritations.
